name: Seoul
description: Seoul is a city where centuries-old palaces and temples sit alongside cutting-edge architecture and design districts. From the winding alleys of Bukchon to the bold curves of the DDP, Seoul's architectural landscape tells a story of rapid transformation and cultural layering.

images.alt: Seoul skyline with traditional and modern architecture

name: Tokyo
description: Tokyo is an ever-evolving laboratory of architecture, where metabolist visions, minimalist residences, and hyper-dense urban fabric coexist. The city's relentless cycle of demolition and construction makes it one of the most architecturally dynamic cities on earth.

images.alt: Tokyo urban landscape with mixed architectural styles

name: Barcelona
description: Barcelona is synonymous with architectural innovation, from Gaudí's organic masterpieces to the geometric precision of the Eixample grid. The city is a living museum of Art Nouveau, Gothic, and contemporary design.

images.alt: Barcelona architecture featuring Gaudí and modernist buildings

name: Zürich
description: Zürich is a quiet powerhouse of architectural discourse, home to ETH and a tradition of restrained, materially honest design. The city and its surrounding region have produced some of the most influential architects of the past century.

images.alt: Zürich cityscape with Swiss precision architecture
